questions.question: Which among the following are two most important elements that differentiate weather conditions?
questions.explanation: Temperature and precipitation are the two most important elements that differentiate weather conditions. They determine the rainfall and others.

questions.question: The regions of mediterranean climate experience which of the following types of rainfall in winter?
questions.explanation: The Mediterranean region of Europe typically experiences hot, dry summers while most of the precipitation falls during the winter months. The Mediterranean region experiences Cyclonic rainfall during winter and the Orographic effect enhances the cyclonic rainfall.

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest contributor to land degradation in India?
questions.explanation: The top layer of the soil is generally carried away by the splash of water during rainfall or floods. Rainfall and the surface runoff which may result from rainfall, produces four main types of soil erosion: splash erosion, sheet erosion, rill erosion, and gully erosion.

questions.question: In which of the following cases, the ecological pyramid is never inverted?
questions.explanation: Pyramid of energy is never inverted.

questions.question: Which of the following gas contributes the maximum to the phenomena of global warming?
questions.explanation: 72% of the totally emitted greenhouse gases is carbon dioxide (CO2). Carbon dioxide emissions therefore are the most important cause of global warming. CO2 is inevitably created by burning fuels like e.g. oil, natural gas, diesel, organic-diesel, petrol, organic-petrol, ethanol.

questions.question: Estuaries possess distinct blooms of excessive growth.Which of the following is the name given to these blooms? (UPSC Prelims 1998)
questions.explanation: Red tides are a special type of blooms, which are found at the estuaries. These blooms are pigmented dinoflagellates. These single celled organisms are able to swim short distances. When the algae present in high concentration, the water appears purple or pink in colour.

questions.question: The water pollution in river is measured by the dissolved amount of which of the following impurities? (UPSC Prelims 1998)
questions.explanation: Water pollution in river or any other water body is measured by amount of dissolved oxygen. Water pollution can be measured on the basis of parameters like dirtyness, change in texture, total suspended solid and other important factors like, 1. Decrease in dissolved oxygen (DO) (concentration of dissolved oxygen in water samples). 2. Increase in BOD, (Biochemical Oxygen Demand) it is a chemical procedure for determining the amount of dissolved oxygen needed by aerobic biological organisms in a body of water to break down organic material present in a given water sample at certain temperature over a specific time period. 3. Increase in COD, (Chemical Oxygen Demand) test is commonly used to indirectly measure the amount of organic compounds in water.

questions.question: Which schedule of the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972 specifies endemic plants which are prohibited from cultivation and planting?
questions.explanation: The schedule VI of the Wildlife Protection Act of 1972 specifies endemic plants which are prohibited from cultivation and planting. Schedule V includes the animals which may be hunted.

questions.question: The Stockholm Convention is related to which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Stockholm convention signed in 2001 was effective from 2004 and is related to the aims to curb the production and use of Persistant Organic Pollutants

questions.question: Which of the following components are considered as the Abiotic components?1) Non-green plants2) Symbionts3) WaterSelect the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The environment around us is not static. Both biotic and abiotic factors are in flux and keeps changing continuously. Some of the Abiotic components are Energy, Radiation, Temperature & heat flow, Water, Atmospheric gases and wind, etc
